personas.label: Fund partner / IC
personas.description: Fund strategy, investment thesis, portfolio construction, LP relations

personas.label: Tax director
personas.description: Fund-level structuring, investor tax, credits, exit optimization

personas.label: Deal Team
personas.description: Deal origination, due diligence across all dimensions, deal execution

personas.label: Portfolio Operations / Asset Management
personas.description: Value creation, portco oversight, operational improvement

personas.label: CFO / Fund controller
personas.description: Fund accounting, NAV, waterfall modeling, financial reporting, audit

personas.label: Investor relations
personas.description: Fundraise support, LP communications, market positioning

phases.label: Structure & Fundraise
phases.tooltip: Defining the fund thesis, structuring the vehicle, selecting domicile, and raising capital from LPs. Fund-level tax planning and investor structuring are established here.

phases.label: Finance & Plan
phases.tooltip: Deploying capital into deals - sourcing, screening, conducting due diligence, structuring transactions, and closing. This is where the investment thesis meets execution.

phases.label: Capital Deployment
phases.tooltip: Completing acquisitions, executing post-deal integration, standing up portfolio company governance, and initiating value creation plans. The transition from deal team to portfolio operations happens here.

phases.label: Operate
phases.tooltip: Driving portfolio company performance, overseeing capital projects, managing risk across the portfolio, and reporting to LPs. Ongoing tax optimization and compliance monitoring continue throughout.

phases.label: Dispose & Wind Down
phases.tooltip: Preparing assets for exit, executing sales or IPOs, optimizing exit tax strategy, managing final distributions, and winding down the fund. Track record crystallizes in this phase.

categories.title: Fund strategy & capital allocation

items.name: Fund strategy & investment thesis
q: Is our investment thesis differentiated, and can we articulate it in a way that resonates with target LPs?
Are we disciplined about staying within our mandate, or drifting into opportunistic deals?

items.name: Portfolio construction & allocation
q: Is our portfolio construction aligned with our fund thesis and LP expectations for diversification?
Are we concentrating risk in ways we’ve explicitly decided to, or inadvertently?

items.name: Sector & market screening
q: Are we originating opportunities with proprietary relationships, or relying on inbound deal flow and competitive processes?
Are we seeing the best deals in our target sectors, or missing opportunities competitors are finding?

items.name: Fund fundraise & LP capital raising
q: Are we positioned competitively for the next fund raise - what’s our track record story and how does it compare to peers?
Are we targeting the right LP segments with the right fund terms, and is our marketing material compelling?

items.name: LP & investor reporting
q: Are our LP reports meeting rising expectations for transparency, frequency, and ESG disclosure?
Can we produce ad-hoc LP requests quickly without a fire drill?

categories.risk: Raising a fund without a differentiated thesis, or deploying capital into opportunities that don’t match LP expectations - eroding credibility for the next fundraise before the current one is fully deployed.
lq: Is our investment thesis differentiated enough to win capital in a competitive fundraise - and can we demonstrate the track record to back it up?
Are we deploying capital with the discipline to say no when an opportunity doesn’t fit the thesis, even when deal flow is thin?

categories.title: Structuring, tax & incentives

items.name: Fund structuring & domicile
q: Is our fund structure optimized for our target investor base, or carrying unnecessary complexity?
Would a different domicile or vehicle type improve tax efficiency or LP access?

items.name: Investor tax considerations
q: Are we addressing the specific tax needs of each investor type - pension, SWF, HNWI, retail?
Could our structure inadvertently create UBTI or other issues for tax-exempt investors?

items.name: Cross-border treaty & withholding planning
q: Are we minimizing withholding tax leakage across jurisdictions?
Are our treaty positions documented and defensible under audit?

items.name: Tax credits & incentives
q: Are we capturing all available credits at the asset level and flowing them efficiently to investors?
Are we structured to take advantage of transferability and direct pay provisions?

items.name: Grants, abatements & exemptions
q: Are location-based incentives factored into our underwriting for each deal?
Are we structured to retain incentives through changes in ownership or operations?

items.name: JV & co-investment structuring
q: Are our co-investment structures aligned with partner objectives while protecting our economics?
Are LP co-invest rights creating complexity in deal execution?

items.name: Tax optimization & transfer pricing
q: Are intercompany arrangements across our portfolio defensible and optimized?
Are we reviewing transfer pricing as portfolio companies grow and evolve?

items.name: Cost segregation analysis
q: Have we accelerated depreciation on acquired assets through cost segregation?
Are there older acquisitions where a retroactive study would unlock cash flow?

items.name: Exit tax planning & optimization
q: Are we planning exit tax strategy early enough to maximize after-tax proceeds?
Have we modeled the tax impact of different exit structures - sale, IPO, recapitalization?

items.name: Tax equity advisory
q: Are we leveraging tax equity structures to monetize credits and improve investor returns?
Is our tax equity partner getting what they need, and are we retaining maximum upside?

items.name: REIT & fund vehicle compliance
q: Are we maintaining REIT qualification without constraining investment flexibility?
Are compliance monitoring processes robust enough to catch issues before they become problems?

categories.risk: Fund structures that create friction with target investors, or asset-level tax strategies that don’t flow benefits efficiently to LPs - leaving value trapped between layers of the structure.
lq: Is our fund structure creating barriers for the LP segments we most want to attract - and would a restructuring unlock meaningful new capital?
Are we capturing tax value at the asset level and flowing it through to investors efficiently, or is value leaking between layers of our structure?

categories.title: Financing & transactions

items.name: Equity raise & lead advisory
q: Are we raising at the right valuation with the right partners?
Is our capital raising timeline creating pressure that leads to suboptimal terms?

items.name: Debt raise & capital markets
q: Is our leverage appropriate for the asset risk profile?
Have we stress-tested covenant headroom under realistic downside scenarios?

items.name: Financial due diligence
q: Are the seller’s financials telling the full story, or are there normalization adjustments we’re missing?

Does the asset demonstrate key attributes of infrastructure - barriers to entry, competitive moat, asset intensity, long-term contracts, minimal volumetric or price risk, inflation linkage, and downside protection?

items.name: Tax due diligence
q: What tax exposures are embedded in this target, and how do they affect post-close economics?
Are there tax attributes - NOLs, credits, step-ups - we can capture in the deal?

items.name: Commercial due diligence
q: Is the revenue base sustainable - are customer contracts, market position, and demand drivers robust?
What’s the competitive moat, and how durable is it over our hold period?

items.name: Technical due diligence
q: What’s the true CapEx requirement to maintain or improve this asset to our standards?
Are there hidden technical risks - deferred maintenance, code violations, capacity limits?

items.name: Valuation
q: Is our valuation methodology consistent across the portfolio and defensible to LPs and auditors?
Are we anchoring to comparable transactions, or building bottom-up with defensible assumptions?

items.name: Market entry & structuring
q: Do we understand the regulatory, competitive, and operational landscape of this market?
Is our entry vehicle and structure right for this jurisdiction and asset class?

items.name: Deal structuring & SPA advisory
q: Are our deal protections - reps, warranties, indemnities - calibrated to the risk?
Are we leaving value on the table in the SPA, or creating friction that could kill the deal?

items.name: Post-deal integration
q: Do we have a value creation plan from day one, and who is accountable for execution?
Are we integrating at the right pace - fast enough to capture value, slow enough to avoid disruption?

items.name: Lender & investor technical advisory
q: Are our lenders getting the independent assurance they need to maintain confidence?
Is our technical reporting aligned with what lenders actually want to see?

categories.risk: Overpaying for an asset, missing diligence red flags, or closing on terms that erode returns over the hold period - turning a good thesis into a bad investment.
lq: Are we underwriting to a base case that would survive scrutiny from our toughest LP - or are we relying on assumptions that make the deal work on paper?
If diligence surfaces a material issue at the eleventh hour, do we have the discipline to walk away or renegotiate rather than close a compromised deal?

categories.title: Governance, risk & compliance

items.name: Fund governance & compliance
q: Is our fund governance keeping pace with regulatory expectations and LP scrutiny?
Are our compliance processes scalable as AUM grows?

items.name: Regulatory & governance compliance
q: Are we ahead of evolving regulatory requirements, or at risk of being caught off guard?
Do we have a clear view of our compliance obligations across every jurisdiction we operate in?

items.name: Stakeholder & LP management
q: Are we managing LP relationships proactively, or only when it’s time to fundraise?
Do our LPs feel like partners, or are they surprised by developments?

items.name: ESG & sustainability reporting
q: Can we demonstrate ESG impact with data, or are our commitments still aspirational?
Are we meeting the ESG reporting standards our LPs are increasingly requiring?

items.name: Risk management framework
q: Do we have a portfolio-wide risk view, or are we managing risk asset by asset?
Are we stress-testing the portfolio against correlated risks - rate changes, regulation, geopolitics?

items.name: Portfolio company governance & oversight
q: Are our portfolio company boards effective, and do we have the right level of oversight?
Are we getting the information we need from portcos quickly enough to act on it?

items.name: Cybersecurity & data privacy
q: Is cybersecurity a board-level topic for our portfolio companies? Where are we most exposed?
Have we assessed cyber risk across the portfolio, and do we have incident response plans?

categories.risk: Regulatory surprises, LP trust erosion, or portfolio-level risk concentrations that aren’t visible until it’s too late - threatening fund performance and the GP’s reputation.
lq: If a major regulatory change hit our portfolio tomorrow, do we have the governance infrastructure to assess exposure and respond across all assets in weeks, not months?
Are we managing LP relationships with enough transparency that bad news is a conversation, not a crisis?

categories.title: Portfolio value creation

items.name: Operational improvement & value creation
q: Do we have a repeatable playbook for driving EBITDA improvement across portfolio companies?
Are we tracking value creation initiatives with the same rigor as the investment thesis?

items.name: Capital project oversight
q: Are our portfolio companies’ capital projects on time and budget?
Who is providing independent oversight, and are they flagging issues early enough?

items.name: Procurement strategy & cost management
q: Are we capturing procurement synergies across the portfolio?
Are portfolio companies buying independently when collective purchasing power could save millions?

items.name: Workforce strategy & capability building
q: Do our portfolio companies have the management teams they need to execute the value creation plan?
Where are the talent gaps that are limiting performance?

items.name: Digital enablement & analytics
q: Are we investing in digital capabilities that drive measurable value, not just modernization?
Can we demonstrate ROI on digital investments to our LPs?

items.name: Operational turnaround & restructuring
q: When an asset underperforms, how quickly can we diagnose the problem and deploy a fix?
Do we have the bench strength and playbook to execute a turnaround, or do we need outside help?

categories.risk: Acquiring assets with a value creation thesis but lacking the operational capability or discipline to execute it - leaving underwriting assumptions unrealized at exit.
lq: Do we have a repeatable value creation playbook that works across asset types, or are we reinventing the approach with every deal?
Are we measuring value creation with the same rigor we apply to underwriting - and holding ourselves accountable when initiatives fall short?

categories.title: Technology & data

items.name: Digital enablement & analytics
q: Are we using data to make better investment and portfolio management decisions?
What decisions are we making on instinct that data could improve?

items.name: Data governance & reporting
q: Can we produce reliable, auditable data across the portfolio on demand?
Are data definitions and reporting standards consistent across portfolio companies?

items.name: Waterfall & distribution modeling
q: Is our waterfall model robust enough to handle complex multi-tier structures and edge cases?
Have we stress-tested distributions under multiple performance scenarios?

items.name: Portfolio analytics & benchmarking
q: Are we benchmarking portfolio company performance against the right peers?
Can we identify underperformance early enough to intervene before it affects fund returns?

items.name: AI governance & responsible AI frameworks
q: As our portfolio companies adopt AI, do we have a governance framework to manage the risks?
Are we positioned to capture AI upside while protecting against reputational and regulatory risk?

categories.risk: Operating with fragmented data across the portfolio, making it impossible to see the full picture or report with confidence - undermining LP trust and decision-making speed.
lq: Can we produce a reliable, portfolio-wide performance view on demand - or does every reporting cycle require manual reconciliation across disconnected systems?
Are we investing in technology at the fund level that will scale with AUM growth, or patching together tools that will break under the weight of the next fund?

categories.title: Asset performance & exit

items.name: Asset management strategy
q: Are we managing each asset for maximum exit value, or just steady-state performance?
Is our hold period strategy still right, or has the market moved?

items.name: Energy management, efficiency & resilience
q: Is energy cost and resilience a competitive advantage or a vulnerability for our assets?
Are we capturing energy-related incentives and credits at the asset level?

items.name: Financial reporting & technical accounting
q: Are our reporting processes efficient enough to support timely LP reporting and audit cycles?
Are we applying technical accounting standards consistently across the portfolio?

items.name: Fund accounting & NAV reporting
q: Is our NAV calculation methodology consistent, defensible, and meeting LP expectations?
Can we explain NAV movements clearly when LPs ask?

items.name: Internal cost audit & benchmarking
q: Are we auditing the right things at the right frequency across the portfolio?
Are audit findings leading to action, or sitting in a report?

items.name: Credit substantiation & audit readiness
q: Can we defend every credit claimed across the portfolio with proper documentation?
If a portfolio company is audited, are they ready - and are we?

items.name: Exit preparation & vendor due diligence
q: Are we starting exit preparation early enough to maximize competitive tension and proceeds?
Would a buyer’s diligence find issues we haven’t already identified and addressed?

items.name: Wind down & fund dissolution
q: Do we have a clear wind-down plan that addresses tail liabilities and LP expectations?
Are we managing the final distributions and reporting with the same rigor as the rest of the fund lifecycle?

categories.risk: Starting exit preparation too late, leaving value on the table, or getting surprised by issues a buyer’s diligence uncovers first - compressing proceeds and damaging track record.
lq: Are we actively managing each asset toward an exit thesis from acquisition - or do we start thinking about exit when the fund timeline forces it?
If a buyer ran diligence on our top asset today, would they find issues we haven’t already identified and addressed?
